title: What's new for AI on GKE: Training, serving, and agents
summary: Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) has introduced innovations for AI training, serving, reinforcement learning, and agentic workflows. The platform now addresses evolving AI infrastructure requirements including cost efficiency, scalability, and reliability for complex workloads beyond frontier model serving.
writeup: This video surveys recent additions to Google Kubernetes Engine for AI work, spanning training, model serving, reinforcement learning, and agentic workflows. It frames these updates as responses to shifting AI infrastructure demands, with emphasis on cost efficiency, scalability, and reliability for complex workloads that extend past frontier model serving. The throughline: GKE is positioned to handle a broader range of AI patterns, including reinforcement learning and agent orchestration, rather than serving alone.
